scan_regions.name: western_desert_egypt
scan_regions.notes: Known buried river channels, Roman-era sites

scan_regions.name: rub_al_khali
scan_regions.notes: Arabian Empty Quarter, possible Ubar-era trade routes

scan_regions.name: taklamakan
scan_regions.notes: Silk Road sites, buried Buddhist settlements

scan_regions.name: sahel_mali_niger
scan_regions.notes: Garamantian-era settlements, ancient Lake Chad shorelines

imagery.notes: More images = better variance. 12 preferred for robust statistics with seasonal coverage. Both ascending and descending orbits give different look angles on the same features.

scan_types.name: temporal_variance_99
scan_types.description: Temporal variance of VV+VH, top 1% anomalies. Primary detector.
scan_types.method: Per-pixel var(VV) + var(VH) over time stack. Threshold at 99th percentile.
scan_types.detects: Anything causing radar return to differ from surrounding desert across passes: buried walls, foundations, filled channels, compacted surfaces, large subsurface voids.

scan_types.name: temporal_variance_999
scan_types.description: Temporal variance top 0.1% only. Highest-confidence candidates.
scan_types.method: Same formula, threshold at 99.9th percentile.
scan_types.detects: Only the strongest anomalies. Very few false positives.

scan_types.name: temporal_cv
scan_types.description: Coefficient of variation -- normalized variance. Catches anomalies in both bright and dark areas.
scan_types.method: CV = std(VV)/mean(VV) + std(VH)/mean(VH). High CV = inconsistent scattering over time relative to brightness.
scan_types.detects: Subsurface features in areas with varying overall brightness. Standard variance is biased toward bright areas; CV normalizes for that.

scan_types.name: temporal_mad
scan_types.description: Median absolute deviation -- robust variance. Ignores outlier images.
scan_types.method: MAD = median(|VV - median(VV)|) + median(|VH - median(VH)|). Robust to single bad acquisitions.
scan_types.detects: Same features as variance but resistant to outlier images. If variance and MAD agree, high confidence. If they disagree, the variance hit was probably an outlier.

scan_types.name: seasonal_difference
scan_types.description: Dry season minus wet season mean. Detects moisture-dependent features.
scan_types.method: Split images into dry/wet by date. Compute mean_dry - mean_wet for VV and VH.
scan_types.detects: Buried paleochannels, old irrigation networks, foundations with different moisture retention than surrounding soil.

config.notes: Adjust per region. These defaults are for Northern Hemisphere deserts.

scan_types.name: crosspol_ratio
scan_types.description: Cross-polarization ratio anomaly. Detects scattering mechanism differences.
scan_types.method: Compute mean(VH/VV) over time per pixel. Anomalous ratio = different scattering geometry than surroundings.
scan_types.detects: Buried structures with different surface roughness or material composition than surrounding soil. Walls and foundations that cause double-bounce or volume scattering.

scan_types.name: crosspol_ratio_variance
scan_types.description: Temporal variance of the VH/VV ratio. Detects features where scattering mechanism fluctuates.
scan_types.method: Compute VH/VV per pixel per image, then temporal variance of that ratio.
scan_types.detects: Features that change scattering type between passes -- e.g. a buried channel that sometimes fills with moisture.

scan_types.name: texture_glcm
scan_types.description: Grey-Level Co-occurrence Matrix texture on mean backscatter. Detects spatial structure.
scan_types.method: Compute GLCM features (contrast, dissimilarity, homogeneity, energy, correlation) on the mean_VV image using a sliding window.
scan_types.detects: Regular geometric patterns: building grids, wall foundations, road networks, field boundaries.
scan_types.score_formula: glcm_contrast + glcm_dissimilarity (both normalized, anomaly = deviation from local background)

scan_types.name: edge_detection
scan_types.description: Edge detection on mean backscatter. Detects linear features.
scan_types.method: Apply Sobel filter to mean_VV and mean_VH. Post-process: Hough transform to find straight-line segments.
scan_types.detects: Linear buried features: walls, roads, canals, aqueducts, field boundaries, fortification lines.
scan_types.score_formula: sqrt(sobel_x^2 + sobel_y^2) for VV and VH, combined

outputs_extra.linear_features.geojson: Detected line segments with orientation and length

scan_types.name: spatial_autocorrelation
scan_types.description: Local Moran's I -- detects spatial clustering of high/low backscatter values.
scan_types.method: Compute local Moran's I statistic on mean_VV with a spatial weights matrix.
scan_types.detects: Isolated anomalous features surrounded by homogeneous terrain. Clustered anomalies (e.g. a settlement).
scan_types.score_formula: Local Moran's I statistic (z-score), focus on outlier quadrants (HL, LH)

scan_types.name: multitemporal_change
scan_types.description: Pairwise change detection -- finds pixels that changed significantly between any two dates.
scan_types.method: For every pair of images (or consecutive pairs), compute absolute difference in VV per pixel.
scan_types.detects: Features that appear/disappear with conditions -- subsurface channels visible only when moisture conditions are right.
scan_types.score_formula: max(abs(VV_i - VV_j)) over all pairs i,j -- per pixel

scan_types.name: backscatter_intensity
scan_types.description: Mean backscatter intensity map. Not anomaly detection -- a baseline reference layer.
scan_types.method: Just the temporal mean of VV and VH. Output as a map.
scan_types.detects: Surface properties: soil moisture, surface roughness, rock outcrops, sand vs gravel. Essential context for interpreting anomaly results.
scan_types.notes: No candidates output. Produces intensity_map.tif only.

scan_types.name: coherence_coh12
scan_types.description: CARD-COH12 12-day coherence. Detects surface stability changes.
scan_types.method: Download pre-computed 12-day coherence from CDSE. Combine with variance: high variance + high coherence = subsurface feature.
scan_types.detects: Discriminates real subsurface features from surface noise. Most useful as a filter on variance results.
scan_types.notes: Disabled by default -- requires additional API requests. Enable for high-priority regions.

scan_types.name: insar_displacement
scan_types.description: Full InSAR displacement from SLC data via ISCE2.
scan_types.method: Download SLC products, run ISCE2 topsStack for coregistration + interferogram + phase unwrapping + geocoding.
scan_types.detects: Subsidence over underground voids (tombs, tunnels, collapsed chambers). Differential compaction.
scan_types.score_formula: abs(displacement_los) where persistent across multiple pairs
scan_types.extra_data_needed: SLC products from CDSE Catalogue (large download, separate processing)
scan_types.notes: Disabled by default -- expensive. Enable for tiles with high-confidence candidates. Requires ISCE2 installed.

scan_types.name: speckle_divergence
scan_types.description: Speckle Divergence Index: ratio of observed variance to expected speckle variance. SDI >> 1 = real physical change, not just noise.
scan_types.method: SDI = var_observed / (mean^2 / N_images). Expected speckle variance for N GRD looks = mean^2/N. Pixels with SDI >> 1 have real temporal change beyond what speckle alone produces.
scan_types.detects: Only genuine physical changes: buried structures affecting surface moisture/roughness differently across time. Filters out the majority of speckle-induced false positives that plague raw variance.

scan_types.name: db_variance
scan_types.description: Variance in decibel domain. SAR amplitude follows Rayleigh/Gamma distribution; dB transform makes it more Gaussian, revealing subtle features hidden in linear domain.
scan_types.method: Delta method: var_dB = (10/ln10)^2 * var_linear / mean^2. High dB variance = feature whose backscatter changes noticeably over time relative to its brightness.
scan_types.detects: Subtle subsurface features in both bright and dark areas. Raw variance is biased toward bright pixels; dB variance normalizes for that while preserving the multiplicative nature of SAR signal.

scan_types.name: moisture_differential
scan_types.description: Soil Moisture Differential: detects where VV and VH temporal behaviors diverge. VV is sensitive to dielectric constant (moisture); VH responds to roughness/volume scattering.
scan_types.method: Score = |log10(var_VV / var_VH)|. Where VV varies over time but VH stays stable, the change is likely dielectric (moisture-retention anomaly from buried structures).
scan_types.detects: Buried walls, foundations, and channels that retain or shed moisture differently than surrounding soil. These create VV-only temporal signatures because VV responds to dielectric constant while VH does not.

scan_types.name: depolarization_fraction
scan_types.description: Depolarization Fraction anomaly: VH/(VV+VH) measures what fraction of incident radar energy is scattered into the cross-pol channel.
scan_types.method: Compute depol = mean_VH / (mean_VV + mean_VH), then anomaly = |depol - local_median|. Double-bounce scattering (dihedral: wall+ground) has different depol than single-bounce (flat desert).
scan_types.detects: Buried right-angle structures (wall foundations, building corners) that create dihedral reflectors. Also detects rubble fields and collapsed chambers that cause volume scattering.

scan_types.name: local_contrast
scan_types.description: Local Contrast Ratio: pixel/local_mean in log domain. Highlights targets that stand out from their immediate neighborhood.
scan_types.method: For VV and VH: compute |log(pixel/local_mean)| with a sliding window. Features that are brighter or darker than surroundings score high, regardless of absolute brightness.
scan_types.detects: Small isolated structures (tombs, wells, cisterns) and sharp boundaries (walls, roads) that create local backscatter anomalies against homogeneous terrain.

scan_types.name: hotspot_cluster
scan_types.description: Multi-scan-type hotspot clustering. Identifies locations where candidates from multiple independent scan types agree spatially.
scan_types.method: Load all candidates from other scan types, cluster with DBSCAN or KD-tree. Hotspot = location where >= N independent scan types flagged anomalies within a radius.
scan_types.detects: Highest-confidence archaeological candidates. When temporal, polarimetric, and spatial methods independently flag the same spot, probability of real subsurface feature is high.

scan_types.name: ml_detector
scan_types.description: Future: trained model for anomaly classification.
scan_types.method: Feed stack.tif to a trained CNN/ViT. Output per-pixel probability of subsurface feature.
scan_types.detects: Whatever the model is trained on. Could combine signals that no single scan type captures.
scan_types.notes: Not implemented. Requires labeled training data from confirmed sites. Placeholder.

composite_scores.name: combined_top5
composite_scores.description: Weighted combination of the strongest independent scan types.
composite_scores.method: Normalize each score to 0-1 across tile. Combined = weighted sum. Candidate must appear in at least 2 types.

api_limits.notes: Free tier. Commercial account removes monthly caps.
